messages.subject: [R-gui] Tinn-R 1.18.2.0 released
messages.body_plain:
Dear Tinn-R user,

We would like to announce that a new version of the Tinn-R for Windows
(1.18.2.0) was released and is available on SourceForge server:
http://sourceforge.net/projects/tinn-r.

Tinn-R is a R script editor under OS Windows.

To see the changes, please, read: 'Help/This version/English/Changes.txt'.

Briefly it will be available also on SciViews server: http://www.sciviews.org/Tinn-R

Regards,
-- 
Tinn-R Team

messages.subject: [R-gui] New version of pmg using RGtk2 package
messages.subject_clean: [R-gui] New version of pmg using RGtk2 package
messages.body_plain:
Dear All,

I've ported over my pmg GUI to RGtk2, the R bindings for GTK2 provided
by Michael Lawrence. PMG is a simple menu-driven GUI, ala RCommander,
written using the GTK toolkit. PMG is not as feature rich as
RCommander, but has some differences such as drag and drop. The main
advantages of moving to RGtk2 are cross-patform portability, and a
significantly enhanced toolkit. RGtk2 is at http://www.ggobi.org/RGtk2

This new port rewrote the backend of pmg to use the iwidget API of
Simon Urbanek. This API makes creating new dialogs relatively
easy. The new iWidgetsRGtk package provides this API and many
extensions to it. These extensions incorporate most of the SVDIalogs
suggested for R GUIs in the SciViews documentation. It is my hope that
people will find this package useful, even if they have not interest
in the pmg package.

The packages are found at http://www.math.csi.cuny.edu/pmg.

The iWidgetsRGtk package has a lengthy vignette detailing how the
package may be used.

I'd appreciate any feedback. These packages haven't been tested too
much and I'd be thankful for bug reports.

John
-- 
o---------------------------------------------------------------------o
|  John Verzani                            verzani at math.csi.cuny.edu  |
|  CUNY/CSI			       www.math.csi.cuny.edu/verzani  |
|  Dept. of Mathematics, 1S-215	                        718 982 3623  |
|  Staten Island, NY 10314	       	          (fax) 718 982 3631  |
o---------------------------------------------------------------------o

messages.subject: [R-gui] Cairo graphics device for Windows and RGtk2 2.8.2 released
messages.subject_clean: [R-gui] Cairo graphics device for Windows and RGtk2 2.8.2 released
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

I have managed to build the Cairo R graphics device for Windows. This 
lets you embed R graphics in RGtk2 interfaces on Windows.

I have also uploaded a minor bugfix release of RGtk2.
Changes:
- New demo showing integration with Cairo graphics device
- Some demo fixes, bugs reported by Bill Morphet.
- Fixed an RGtkDataFrame bug reported by John Verzani.

Please download new versions at http://www.ggobi.org/rgtk2

Thanks,
Michael Lawrence
messages.body_snippet:

Chang

messages.subject: [R-gui] Tinn-R 1.18.4.3 released
messages.body_plain:
Dear Tinn-R user,

We would like to announce that a new version of the Tinn-R for Windows
(1.18.4.3) was released and is available on SourceForge server:
http://sourceforge.net/projects/tinn-r.

Tinn-R is a R script editor under *OS Windows*.

Two useful tools for file conversion were added:
- deplate (http://deplate.sourceforge.net/index.php) and
- txt2tags (http://txt2tags.sourceforge.net).

For *deplate* was proposed the extension '.dlpt' and for
*txt2tags* Tinn-R recognizes '.t2t'. The basic highilighters, based in XML,
were proposed for both, we hope the users help us in the development.

Also, the interface for syntax colors preferences for both are different
of the already known Tinn-R interface. So, consider that it is working
nice but will be temporary.

Tinn-R now enables you to compile LaTeX files (MikTeX, http://www.miktex.org)
and view the DVI and PDF results (Yap and Acrobat) and also to see 'HTML'
files in your preferred browser starting from Tinn-R.

To see all changes, please, read: 'Help/English/Changes (html)'.

Briefly it will be available also on SciViews server: http://www.sciviews.org/Tinn-R

Regards,
-- 
Tinn-R Team
